management_notes:
Xali Gold Corp.'s management team has navigated a significant strategic pivot over the past 12-18 months. The company's most advanced asset — the El Oro Tailings Project in Mexico — was divested to Kappes, Cassiday and Associates (KCA) in November 2025 via a Definitive Agreement, effectively exiting the company's only NI 43-101 compliant resource. Simultaneously, the company has been pursuing the Sarape Project in Sonora, Mexico (optioned February 2025 from Advanced Lithium Corp.) and maintaining South American exploration interests.

The El Oro tailings divestiture to KCA followed an 18-month KCA drilling and metallurgical study that confirmed the resource quality (78% Au and 86% Ag recovery rates, ~10-15% resource size increase). Transferring the project to KCA — who conducted the technical work — suggests the company determined it could not self-fund development and accepted a deal that monetises the asset without requiring construction capital. The terms of the divestiture (what Xali received in exchange) were not confirmed in detail from sources reviewed.

Specific insider ownership data was not confirmed through SEDAR+ filings reviewed for this research. Score: 2/5. Management is executing a pivot toward earlier-stage exploration while divesting the company's most de-risked asset.
geology_notes:
Xali Gold's geological position as of April 2026 is primarily speculative exploration following the El Oro Tailings divestiture. The Sarape Project in Sonora, Mexico was optioned in February 2025 from Advanced Lithium Corp. The 57 km² property is located in the Rio Sonora Valley, within the same district as First Majestic Silver's Santa Elena Mine, Premier Gold Mines' Mercedes Mine (now Endeavour Silver's), and SilverCrest's Las Chispas Mine (acquired by Coeur Mining for CA$2.4 billion in 2022). The geological neighbourhood is world-class epithermal silver-gold territory.

However, Sarape is at the drill target generation stage. As of July 2025, the company was still developing drill targets at Sarape, with drilling planned for Q4 2025/Q1 2026. There are no NI 43-101 Mineral Resource Estimates for Sarape and no completed drill programme confirming the presence of significant mineralisation. The El Oro Tailings NI 43-101 resource (119,900 oz Au Inferred at 2.94 g/t Au, 3.06M oz Ag Inferred at 75.12 g/t Ag, from a 2014 estimate) was the company's only classified resource, and this asset has been transferred to KCA.

Score: 1/5. No remaining NI 43-101 Mineral Resource Estimate. Sarape is pre-resource exploration at an attractive address (near Las Chispas), but no drilling results are available to confirm mineralisation quality.
capital_notes:
The El Oro Tailings divestiture to KCA in November 2025 would have provided some consideration to Xali Gold — whether in cash, shares in KCA's operating entity, or royalty — but the specific financial terms were not confirmed in sources reviewed for this research. The divestiture eliminates both the asset value and any forward capex obligation for El Oro tailings processing, which is a neutral-to-modestly-positive capital event depending on the terms received.

Xali Gold's share structure is estimated at approximately 280+ million shares outstanding (based on the Sarape option agreement math: 140 million shares = 50% of shares outstanding at signing, February 2025). This is a high share count for a company at the speculative exploration stage with no operating revenue. Multiple staged share issuances under the Sarape option agreement (26.6M, 21M, 91.4M shares across earn-in tranches) will further dilute shareholders as earn-in milestones are hit.

Specific cash position was not confirmed. The combination of a high share count, a divestiture of the primary asset, and ongoing option-based share issuances creates a capital structure that warrants caution. Score: 2/5.
catalyst_notes:
Xali Gold's near-term catalysts are limited to initial drill results from Sarape, which was targeting a Q4 2025/Q1 2026 drill programme based on July 2025 disclosures. If drilling commenced as planned, first assay results could arrive in early-to-mid 2026. Success at Sarape — given its location near Las Chispas, one of the world's highest-grade silver-gold deposits — would be a material positive catalyst.

However, the company divested its most advanced asset in November 2025, resetting the development clock to zero. The South American properties (Peru/Colombia mentioned in July 2025 updates) provide additional optionality but are also at early exploration stage. There is no near-term path to a resource estimate, economic study, or production decision for any Xali asset.

The neighbourhood effect of Sarape (near Las Chispas, Santa Elena) is a positive geological signpost, but location alone does not create a resource. Drill results from Sarape are the only near-term catalyst with meaningful potential. Score: 2/5.
acquisition_notes:
No NAV is calculable for Xali Gold Corp. as of April 2026. The company's only NI 43-101 Mineral Resource Estimate (El Oro Tailings, 2014) has been transferred to KCA as part of the divestiture agreement. The Sarape Project has no resource estimate, no completed drill programme, and no economic study. South American properties are similarly at the drill target or early exploration stage.

The residual value in Xali Gold is the speculative option on: (1) Sarape exploration success at an attractive geological address (near Las Chispas); (2) South American exploration potential; and (3) any consideration received for the El Oro tailings transfer. Without confirmed resource data or economic studies, no P/NAV framework is applicable.

Market cap data for XGC was not confirmed in this research cycle, preventing a meaningful exploration-stage comparables analysis. Score: 1/5 by framework definition.
analyst_summary:
Xali Gold Corp. earns an AVOID with a composite score of 8/25. The investment case has been materially weakened by the November 2025 divestiture of the El Oro Tailings Project — the company's only NI 43-101 classified resource — to Kappes, Cassiday and Associates. This leaves Xali with no validated resource, no economic study, and an exploration portfolio that starts from zero: Sarape (Sonora, Mexico, near Las Chispas) is at the drill target generation stage, and South American properties are similarly early.

The geological address at Sarape is genuinely interesting. The Rio Sonora Valley district produced the Las Chispas Mine (CA$2.4 billion Coeur Mining acquisition in 2022), the Santa Elena Mine (First Majestic), and the Mercedes Mine (Endeavour Silver). Proximity to world-class epithermal silver-gold deposits is a meaningful positive geological signal. If Sarape drilling confirms similar mineralisation, the company could re-rate significantly.

However, as of April 2026, Xali is in a strategic transition with no classified resources, a high share count (~280M+), potential for ongoing dilution from the Sarape earn-in share issuances, and limited confirmed cash. The AVOID rating reflects the current lack of data to make a positive case under the Verdict Framework. The single catalyst to watch is initial Sarape drill results — if these confirm high-grade epithermal mineralisation at Las Chispas-style grades, re-evaluate immediately.
